seo.title: Education Data Extraction AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for data extraction AI agents in education. Covers FERPA, COPPA, State education data privacy laws.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for data extraction in educational environments carries significant legal and ethical obligations, particularly when handling student records protected under FERPA and COPPA. Schema-guided LLM extraction pipelines must be rigorously validated to prevent data leakage, misclassification of sensitive fields, and unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable information (PII) belonging to minors. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls to ensure your extraction architecture meets regulatory requirements and protects institutional integrity.

sections.heading: Data Governance & Consent Management
sections.description: Establish clear data ownership, consent workflows, and access controls before any student data enters the extraction pipeline.

items.label: Verify parental consent records for all minors under 13
items.description: Before processing any document containing data from students under 13, confirm COPPA-compliant parental consent is on file and logged with a timestamp and consent version number.

items.label: Map every extracted schema field to a FERPA education record category
items.description: Document whether each output field (e.g., GPA, IEP status, disciplinary notes) constitutes an 'education record' under FERPA 34 CFR Part 99 and apply corresponding access restrictions.

items.label: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) on extraction pipeline outputs
items.description: Restrict structured output files so only 'school officials with legitimate educational interest' as defined in your FERPA annual notice can query or download results.

items.label: Maintain a data processing agreement (DPA) with all LLM API vendors
items.description: Ensure your LLM provider signs a DPA explicitly prohibiting use of student data for model training, advertising, or any purpose beyond the contracted extraction task.

items.label: Enforce state-specific student data privacy law requirements
items.description: Cross-reference applicable state laws (e.g., California SOPIPA, New York Education Law 2-d, Illinois SOPPA) and add any stricter consent or deletion requirements as pipeline pre-conditions.

items.label: Define and enforce data retention limits on extracted output files
items.description: Set automated deletion schedules for structured output files containing student PII, aligned with your institution's records retention policy and applicable state law timelines.

items.label: Log all data access events to an immutable audit trail
items.description: Record who accessed extracted student records, when, from which IP, and for what stated purpose, retaining logs for a minimum of 5 years to support FERPA compliance audits.

sections.heading: Schema Validation & Extraction Accuracy
sections.description: Ensure the LLM extraction pipeline produces structurally correct, type-consistent, and complete outputs that accurately reflect source documents without hallucination.

items.label: Enforce strict JSON schema validation on every LLM output before persistence
items.description: Use a schema validator (e.g., Pydantic, JSON Schema Draft-07) to reject any extraction response that fails type checks, missing required fields, or violates enum constraints before writing to the database.

items.label: Run field-level accuracy spot checks against ground-truth documents weekly
items.description: Randomly sample 5% of extracted records each week, manually verify at least 10 fields per record against source documents, and track accuracy rates per field type in a quality dashboard.

items.label: Implement completeness validation to flag records with missing required fields
items.description: Define a 'completeness score' for each schema and automatically quarantine any extracted record scoring below 85% for human review before it enters downstream systems.

items.label: Add type consistency checks for date, numeric, and identifier fields
items.description: Validate that fields like date_of_birth follow ISO 8601, student_id matches your institution's format regex, and GPA values fall within the expected 0.0–4.0 numeric range.

items.label: Detect and reject hallucinated values not present in source documents
items.description: Implement a post-extraction grounding check that verifies each extracted value appears verbatim or as a clear inference in the source text, flagging any value with no textual anchor for human review.

items.label: Version-control all extraction schemas and prompt templates
items.description: Store schema definitions and LLM system prompts in Git with semantic versioning; require a documented change review before deploying schema updates that affect PII fields.

items.label: Test extraction pipeline against adversarial and edge-case documents
items.description: Include handwritten documents, multi-language records, scanned PDFs with low OCR confidence, and documents with redacted fields in your test suite to validate graceful failure handling.

sections.heading: PII Detection, Redaction & Minimization
sections.description: Prevent unnecessary exposure of student PII by detecting sensitive fields early in the pipeline and applying minimization and redaction controls.

items.label: Run automated PII detection on all input documents before LLM processing
items.description: Use a PII scanner (e.g., Microsoft Presidio, AWS Comprehend) to identify SSNs, medical record numbers, and financial data in source documents and redact fields not required by the extraction schema.

items.label: Pseudonymize student identifiers in extraction pipeline logs
items.description: Replace student names and IDs with pseudonymous tokens in all pipeline execution logs, retaining a separate encrypted mapping table accessible only to the data privacy officer.

items.label: Exclude special education and IEP data from general extraction schemas
items.description: Treat IEP status, disability classifications, and 504 plan details as a separate high-sensitivity schema requiring additional authorization, separate storage, and stricter access controls.

items.label: Apply data minimization: extract only fields explicitly required by the use case
items.description: Audit the extraction schema quarterly to remove any fields that are no longer consumed by downstream systems, reducing the PII surface area in stored outputs.

items.label: Encrypt all structured output files at rest using AES-256
items.description: Store extracted JSON/CSV output files in encrypted storage with key management handled by a dedicated KMS, ensuring keys are rotated annually and access is logged.

items.label: Mask or truncate sensitive fields in extraction preview interfaces
items.description: In any UI that displays extracted records, mask fields like SSN (show only last 4 digits) and date of birth (show only year) unless the user has an explicit elevated access role.

items.label: Implement automated alerts for bulk extraction of student records
items.description: Trigger a security alert and require supervisor approval when any single API call or batch job attempts to extract records for more than 500 students, preventing unauthorized bulk data pulls.

sections.heading: Bias Auditing, Accessibility & Institutional Safeguards
sections.description: Ensure extracted data and the pipeline itself do not introduce or perpetuate bias, meet accessibility standards, and include institutional oversight mechanisms.

items.label: Audit extraction outputs for demographic bias in field error rates
items.description: Quarterly, stratify extraction accuracy metrics by student race, language, disability status, and socioeconomic indicators to detect if the LLM performs worse for specific demographic groups.

items.label: Ensure all extraction pipeline interfaces comply with Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 AA
items.description: Test all dashboards, review UIs, and error-reporting interfaces used by school staff with automated accessibility scanners (e.g., axe, WAVE) and remediate any Level A or AA failures before deployment.

items.label: Establish a human-in-the-loop review process for high-stakes extracted fields
items.description: Require manual verification by a qualified staff member before extracted fields that influence enrollment decisions, disciplinary records, or special education eligibility are written to authoritative systems.

items.label: Document that the AI extraction tool does not replace qualified instructional staff
items.description: Maintain written policy confirming the extraction pipeline is an administrative tool only, and that no extracted output is used to make autonomous decisions about student instruction without educator review.

items.label: Conduct an annual third-party privacy impact assessment (PIA)
items.description: Engage an independent assessor to evaluate the extraction pipeline against current FERPA, COPPA, and applicable state law requirements, publishing findings to the institution's data governance committee.

items.label: Establish a student/parent data rights request workflow
items.description: Create a documented process for parents and eligible students to request inspection, correction, or deletion of extracted records within the 45-day FERPA response window, with a designated staff owner.

items.label: Train all staff who interact with extraction outputs on FERPA obligations
items.description: Require annual FERPA training completion for any staff member with access to extracted student records, logging completion in your LMS and revoking access for non-compliant users.

items.label: Define and test an incident response plan for extraction data breaches
items.description: Document a breach response playbook specific to student data exposure, including notification timelines (72 hours for state laws, as applicable), affected-party communication templates, and a designated incident commander.

notes.note: Any LLM vendor receiving student education records through the extraction pipeline must qualify as a 'school official' under FERPA or operate under a valid exception. Ensure your vendor contract explicitly limits data use to the extraction service, prohibits re-disclosure, and grants the institution direct control over the data. Failure to establish this relationship before processing makes the disclosure unlawful and exposes the institution to loss of federal funding.

For students under 13, the extraction pipeline must not transmit any personal information to third-party LLM APIs without verifiable parental consent, unless the school provides consent on behalf of parents under the 'school official' exception. Document this consent basis explicitly in your DPA and pipeline configuration, and ensure the LLM provider does not use the data for any commercial purpose beyond the contracted service.

Laws such as New York Education Law 2-d require that any third-party contractor processing student data submit a signed Parents' Bill of Rights for Data Privacy and Security, and prohibit use of student data for targeted advertising or creation of commercial profiles. California SOPIPA similarly bars operators from selling student data or using it to build profiles for non-educational purposes. Verify compliance with every state in which your institution operates before deploying the extraction pipeline.

All interfaces used by school staff to review, correct, or export extracted student data must meet Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act and WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards. This includes keyboard navigability, screen reader compatibility, sufficient color contrast, and accessible error messaging. Non-compliant tools used in federally funded institutions may trigger OCR complaints and jeopardize federal funding streams independent of data privacy violations.

cta.heading: Automate Your Education Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI data extraction pipeline against FERPA, COPPA, and state education privacy requirements—catching schema violations, PII exposures, and compliance gaps before they become institutional liabilities.
